title: asperula gunnii var. pusilla
intro: Asperula gunnii var. pusilla, also known as dwarf woodruff, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing several health benefits such as improving respiratory function and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties, although its use should be in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
